popularStory.headline: Sir William Parr find a grave
popularStory.description:
Son of Sir Thomas Parr of Kendal and Alice Tunstall, he married, after August 1473, as his second wife, Elizabeth FitzHugh, daughter of Henry, 5th Lord FitzHugh of Ravensworth and Alice Neville.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Parr於1435年出生在英國維斯特莫藍德肯達爾時，他的父親Sir Thomas Parr29歲，母親Alice Tunstall31歲。他於大約1467年9月與Joan Trusbutt結婚。他去世於大約1483年在英國倫敦享年49歲，埋葬在英國維斯特莫藍德肯達爾。

nameMeanings.name: Parr
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Parr in Lancashire, or from residence at an enclosure (Middle English parr, Old English pearr).
German: from Middle Low German parre ‘parish, district, minister's house’; a metonymic occupational name for a parson or for someone who worked in a parsonage or manse. Compare Pfarr .
English: variant of Pear with the common late Middle English and early modern English change of -er- to -ar-.

variants.name: Pare
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Henri, Laurent, Normand, Fernand, Alphonse, Clermont, Elphege, Alain, Benoit, Gaston, Girard.
French (Paré): occupational name for someone who finished cloth, from the past participle of parer ‘to prepare’.

variants.name: Parman
meanings: Americanized form of German Parmann: variant of Parr .
Possibly an altered form of German Bärmann (see Barmann ).

variants.name: Porr
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ulrike, Gunther.
metonymic occupational name for a grower or seller of leeks or other vegetables, from Middle High German porre, pforre ‘leek’ (from Latin porrum).

variants.name: Pfarr
meanings: South German: from Middle High German pfarr ‘district, parish’ or pfarre(r) ‘parish priest’, hence an occupational name for a parson, or a topographic name for someone who lived near the parsonage.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Parr was born in 1435, in Kendal, Westmorland, England, his father, Sir Thomas Parr, was 29 and his mother, Alice Tunstall, was 31. He married Joan Trusbutt about September 1467. He died about 1483, in London, England, at the age of 49, and was buried in Kendal, Westmorland, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Parr est né en 1435, à Kendal, Westmorland, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas Parr, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Alice Tunstall, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Joan Trusbutt environ septembre 1467. Il est décédé environ 1483, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterré à Kendal, Westmorland, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Parr(이)가 1435년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 켄다에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas Parr(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Tunstall(은)는 31세였다. 그는 대략 1467년 9월 Joan Trusbutt(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 대략 1483년 영국 런던에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 웨스트모를랜드 켄다에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Parr родился в 1435 г. в местности кендаль, вестморланд, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas Parr было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Tunstall, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Trusbutt около сентябрь 1467 г. Он умер около 1483 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 49 лет и был похоронен в местности кендаль, вестморланд, Англия.
